title: WCAG 2.2 AA hardening + manual assistive-tech audit + print-legibility audit
context: repair-cafe-kits must be usable by everyone, including people with disabilities, and must print legibly since most field use is on paper. WCAG 2.2 AA conformance requires both automated checks and a manual assistive-technology (AT) audit. The AT matrix is: NVDA+Firefox, JAWS+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari, TalkBack+Chrome, and keyboard-only on each desktop browser. Print legibility requires WCAG AA contrast, minimum font size, and no color-only meaning. This task hardens the delivery skeleton and the print layer to meet these requirements and produces a documented sign-off.
objective: Harden the delivery skeleton to WCAG 2.2 AA: fix all critical automated issues, conduct and document a manual AT audit across the full browser/AT matrix, perform a print-legibility audit, and commit the signed-off audit report.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated axe-core and pa11y scans pass with 0 critical issues across all core flows (rendering a checklist, rendering the organizer playbook, the print view)

A manual AT audit is conducted and documented covering: NVDA+Firefox, JAWS+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari, TalkBack+Chrome, and keyboard-only navigation on each desktop browser; all blocking issues found are fixed before sign-off
The manual audit report is committed to the repo, names the qualified reviewer who conducted the audit, and is signed off by that reviewer
The print-legibility audit passes: all printed content meets WCAG AA contrast ratio, minimum 12pt equivalent font size, and no color-only meaning in any print view
The AT audit and print-legibility audit are completed before M1 milestone exit and the sign-off cadence is documented (before each future release)
output: A merged PR with WCAG 2.2 AA fixes, a committed manual AT audit report (full browser/AT matrix, signed off by a qualified reviewer), and a print-legibility audit pass, with 0 critical axe/pa11y issues.
